sourceMovie.title: Nobody
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Nobody (2021) as a action theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The action and thriller blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: John Wick
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: John Wick (2014) is the natural next step after Nobody: same action DNA, different perspective. The tense streak and thriller elevate it well above a simple genre exercise.

recommendations.title: John Wick: Chapter 3 - Parabellum
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Fans of Nobody who respond to its action will find John Wick: Chapter 3 - Parabellum (2019) immediately familiar. Both films share a thriller sensibility without ever feeling like carbon copies of each other.

recommendations.title: Mr. & Mrs. Smith
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: There's a reason Nobody fans keep landing on Mr. & Mrs. Smith (2005) — the action is unmistakably related even when the plots diverge. Both films operate on the same emotional frequency, especially in their third acts.

recommendations.title: The Matrix Revolutions
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The Matrix Revolutions draws from the same action well as Nobody, channeling thriller in ways that feel unmistakably related. The 2003 film makes a natural companion watch — similar in spirit but distinctly its own.

recommendations.title: The Bourne Ultimatum
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The Bourne Ultimatum (2007) captures the action appeal of Nobody without simply imitating it. The thriller streak running through both films is what puts The Bourne Ultimatum at the top of any "if you liked…" shortlist.

recommendations.title: The Bourne Supremacy
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The action appeal of Nobody — that tense edge, the thriller — translates cleanly into The Bourne Supremacy (2004). Different setting, same essential satisfaction.

recommendations.title: Swordfish
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The filmmakers behind Swordfish (2001) clearly understood what makes action work — the same quality that keeps Nobody rewatchable. Expect the same dark rhythm, executed with a fresh angle.

recommendations.title: Dirty Harry
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Dirty Harry (1971) is the kind of film Nobody fans discover and immediately send to their group chat. The action overlap is real, but it's the dark emotional pull that seals the recommendation.

recommendations.title: The Boondock Saints
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The Boondock Saints shares Nobody's gift for action — the 1999 film delivers thriller without overstaying its welcome. Consider it required viewing once you've finished rewatching your first pick.

recommendations.title: You Only Live Twice
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Much of what makes Nobody compelling — the action, the thriller — shows up in You Only Live Twice (1967) in a slightly different form. Both films understand that tense storytelling works best when it trusts the audience.

faq.question: What should I watch after Nobody?
faq.answer: Try John Wick, John Wick: Chapter 3 - Parabellum, Mr. & Mrs. Smith—each shares DNA with Nobody while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Nobody?
faq.answer: Nobody (2021) is best described as Action, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Nobody part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Nobody Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
